problem_solved: Occupational therapists lack specialized practice management software tailored to their unique workflows, including functional assessments, rehabilitation planning, and adaptive equipment tracking
who_buys_it: Occupational therapy practices, rehabilitation centers, and individual OT practitioners in Belgium and Netherlands
key_features: Billing and invoicing for OT services
business_model: SaaS subscription model with tiered pricing based on practice size and number of therapists
why_varden_should_care: Pure-play OT practice management solution in Benelux market; addresses specialized OT workflows not covered by general practice management platforms; strong regional market position with room for expansion

problem_solved: Healthcare providers, including occupational therapists, need integrated practice management solutions that handle scheduling, documentation, billing, and patient communication in one platform
who_buys_it: Physiotherapists, occupational therapists, and other allied health professionals in Belgium
key_features: Care Connect platform for practice management
Electronic health records for therapy documentation
business_model: SaaS subscription with per-user or per-practice pricing; additional fees for premium features and integrations
why_varden_should_care: Established healthcare IT player in Belgium market with strong network effects; platform serves multiple therapy disciplines including OT; opportunity for strategic partnership or regional expansion

problem_solved: Occupational therapists and physiotherapists in Netherlands need localized practice management software that integrates with Dutch healthcare systems and regulatory requirements
who_buys_it: Occupational therapists, physiotherapists, and rehabilitation centers in Netherlands
business_model: SaaS subscription model with pricing based on practice size; includes support and updates
why_varden_should_care: Dutch market leader in OT practice management; strong local market knowledge and regulatory compliance; potential for expansion into broader rehabilitation management

problem_solved: Therapists need efficient way to create and deliver personalized exercise programs, track patient adherence, and monitor rehabilitation progress remotely
who_buys_it: Physiotherapists, occupational therapists, rehabilitation centers, and healthcare systems across Europe
key_features: Custom exercise program builder with AI assistance
Patient app (PhysiApp) for home exercise adherence
Real-time progress monitoring and pain tracking
business_model: Freemium model with 14-day free trial; paid subscription tiers for individual practitioners and enterprise plans for healthcare organizations
why_varden_should_care: Market leader in rehabilitation exercise prescription with strong clinical validation (100+ studies); could expand deeper into OT-specific assessments and functional tracking; acquisition target for companies entering European rehabilitation market

problem_solved: Traditional MSK care is expensive, inaccessible, and often results in unnecessary surgeries; patients need scalable, AI-powered rehabilitation solutions
who_buys_it: Self-insured employers, health plans, and healthcare systems seeking to reduce MSK costs and improve member outcomes
key_features: Digital physical therapy with real-time feedback
Motion-tracking technology via tablet and wearables
business_model: B2B enterprise contracts with employers and health plans; value-based pricing tied to cost savings (3.2:1 ROI validated)
why_varden_should_care: High-growth digital therapeutics unicorn with proven clinical outcomes; strong employer channel relationships; could expand into occupational therapy and return-to-work assessment; represents digital health consolidation opportunity

problem_solved: Healthcare providers struggle with fragmented patient data across multiple systems, making care coordination and clinical documentation inefficient
who_buys_it: Hospitals, clinics, and healthcare networks in France seeking to integrate and streamline their medical documentation workflows
key_features: Lifen Integration: automated DPI (Digital Patient Record) feeding
business_model: B2B SaaS contracts with healthcare organizations; pricing based on document volume and integration complexity
why_varden_should_care: Leading French healthtech company with strong hospital relationships; infrastructure play for healthcare data interoperability; potential channel partner for OT-specific solutions entering French market

problem_solved: Therapists need integrated clinic management that combines scheduling, treatment notes, exercise prescription, and patient communication in one seamless system
who_buys_it: Physiotherapists, sports therapists, podiatrists, and multidisciplinary therapy clinics
business_model: SaaS subscription with pricing tiers based on practice size and features; NHS partnerships for pulmonary rehabilitation programs
why_varden_should_care: Comprehensive therapy practice platform with AI capabilities; growing user base across UK and Europe; could expand OT-specific functional assessment modules; potential acquisition target for practice management consolidation

problem_solved: Healthcare providers need patient engagement and outcomes tracking solutions that integrate with clinical workflows and support value-based care initiatives
who_buys_it: Physical therapy clinics, occupational therapy practices, and healthcare organizations seeking MIPS compliance and value-based care tools
business_model: SaaS subscription with outcomes registry fees; pricing tied to provider count and MIPS reporting needs
why_varden_should_care: Proven platform for MSK patient engagement with outcomes focus; MIPS expertise valuable as value-based care expands; potential for European market expansion with localized regulatory compliance
